conversations.say: Morgan, regarding your request to date the change in the private habit: I have the ledger fragments, but you must strictly honor our attribution limits and protect customer privacy.
conversations.offer: A bounded look at transaction timestamps and collector rotations.
conversations.request: Confirmation of your binding promise not to name any customer as R.

conversations.say: Inez, I agree with your early warning. We must maintain a tight boundary around the service route and customer records so outside investigators don't scapegoat us.
conversations.offer: Full alignment and mutual defense of our informal neighborhood arrangements.
conversations.request: Formal acceptance of our shared protection boundary.

conversations.say: Dee, our shift logs and collector accounts overlap around the service exit. Let's align our timelines so nobody manufactures a false narrative out of our blind spots.
conversations.offer: My shop's shift context and delivery arrival notes.
conversations.request: Your account of the physical gap near the service route.

public_statement: My counter records show multiple collectors and changing routines over the years, not a single static ghost. If anyone wants to examine the ledger, they respect customer privacy first.

commitments.promise: Establish a shared boundary to protect Dee, Rafi, and Manny from being scapegoated over the service route.
Restrict ledger disclosure terms and avoid naming any customer as R.

correction: Clarifying that multiple collectors and shared accounts used the R-associated services across years, which complicates any simple single-author narrative.
goal_progress: Negotiated bounded ledger terms with Morgan.
Accepted Inez's protective boundary proposal for local infrastructure workers.
Aligned shift and timeline notes with Dee.
reasoning_summary: Rafi Calderón solidifies agreements with Inez and Morgan in Section 2, aligning on customer privacy and local worker protections while coordinating timeline observations with Dee.
